item.title: 🇵🇭🌊 Magnitude 7.8 Earthquake Strikes Philippines, Tsunami Warnings Issued
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake hit off the southern Philippines island of Mindanao, killing at least 19 people and triggering tsunami warnings across the Pacific. Waves of up to 1 meter were observed in the Philippines, Indonesia, and Japan.

item.title: ☢️⚔️ SIPRI: All Nine Nuclear-Armed States Expanding Arsenals Amid Global Tensions
item.description: The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute reports that all nine nuclear-armed states are modernizing and expanding their arsenals. India increased its stockpile to 190 warheads, extending its lead over Pakistan which holds 170.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukraine and Russia Trade Air Attacks as Zelenskyy Pushes for Direct Talks
item.description: Russia and Ukraine exchanged deadly air attacks hours after President Zelenskyy met with European leaders to push for direct negotiations. The Ukrainian leader expressed willingness to freeze current battle lines to end the war.

item.title: 🇯🇵💴 Japanese Yen Lingers Above ¥160 to Dollar as BOJ Meeting Approaches
item.description: The yen traded back above ¥160 to the dollar, testing levels not seen in decades as markets await the Bank of Japan policy meeting. The currency remains under pressure from widening US-Japan interest rate differentials.

item.title: 🛢️⛽ OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ approved another modest increase in oil output targets for July, marking the fourth hike since the Strait of Hormuz was disrupted. The move comes despite ongoing supply concerns from the Iran-Israel conflict.

item.title: 🇱🇻 NATO shoots down drone entering Latvia from Russia
item.description: NATO fighter jets shot down a drone that entered Latvian airspace from Russia, the latest in a series of security incidents along Europe's eastern border. The incident highlights ongoing tensions between Russia and NATO allies.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky's European allies set five conditions for peace talks with Russia
item.description: Britain, Germany and France outlined five conditions for Ukraine-Russia peace negotiations following talks with President Zelensky. The conditions come as Zelensky signaled willingness to freeze current battle lines to achieve peace.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenian PM Pashinyan claims victory in parliamentary election
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan claimed victory in Armenia's parliamentary election, with early results showing his pro-Western party leading. The election was seen as a choice between closer ties with Europe or Russia.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices surge more than $4 as Israel-Iran strikes threaten ceasefire
item.description: Oil prices jumped by more than $4 per barrel as Israel conducted strikes on Iran and Lebanon, threatening the fragile ceasefire. Brent crude neared $98 despite OPEC+ approving another output hike.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Expands Nuclear Deterrence Pact to Scandinavia as Norway Opens Talks
item.description: Norway has become the latest ally to discuss joining France's extended nuclear deterrence initiative, as European defense cooperation deepens amid growing security concerns.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Issues Fresh Travel Advisory for Iran, Urges Nationals to Leave Immediately
item.description: India has reiterated its travel advisory for Iran amid escalating regional tensions, advising Indian nationals to avoid travel and urging those present to depart immediately.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Defence Ministers Convene in Nicosia With Ukraine War Set to Dominate Agenda
item.description: The EU's 27 defence ministers are gathering in Cyprus on Monday, with the ongoing Ukraine conflict expected to be the primary focus of discussions.

item.title: 💰 Gold Prices Decline as Middle East Reescalation Dampens Safe-Haven Demand
item.description: Gold prices began the week with losses despite renewed Middle East tensions, as market dynamics shift amid the ongoing Israel-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
item.description: A Ukrainian drone struck a train in Crimea, killing its assistant driver and injuring the driver. The attack highlights ongoing Ukrainian strikes deep in Russian-occupied territories.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping hails new era in China-North Korea ties ahead of Pyongyang summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping said ties with North Korea are at a "new historical starting point" before his arrival in Pyongyang for a rare summit with Kim Jong Un, aiming to counter US influence.

item.title: 🛡️ Chinese warships shadow global executives at Taiwan AI tech summit
item.description: As Nvidia, Intel and other tech leaders gathered for Taiwan's Computex, Chinese military vessels conducted operations nearby, underscoring tensions over the island that hosts the world's most advanced chip production.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran says Hormuz Strait will reopen with transit fees
item.description: Iran's ambassador to Moscow said the strategic Strait of Hormuz will be open but under new conditions set by Iran and Oman, including transit fees, as the conflict with the US continues.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korean President Lee rules out nuclear armament, affirms denuclearization goal
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung stated South Korea cannot abandon the goal of denuclearizing the Korean Peninsula, while also noting North Korea may have felt humiliated by Seoul's alleged drone incursion.

item.title: 🇰🇭⚠️ Cambodia's scam center crackdown fails to dismantle industry, Amnesty reports
item.description: A year-long Cambodian crackdown on online scam centers has failed to dismantle much of the industry and may have harmed victims, according to a new Amnesty International investigation.

item.title: 💵📈 Dollar climbs to two-month peak as Fed rate hike bets ramp up
item.description: The US dollar reached a two-month high after a strong jobs report sent traders increasing bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike this year, while the yen weakened further toward intervention territory.

item.title: 🇨🇳📦 China exports set for strong May on front-loaded orders and chip demand
item.description: China's export growth likely strengthened in May as overseas orders were brought forward to pre-empt energy price pressures from the Gulf war, alongside sustained global demand for semiconductors and AI components.

item.title: 💥 Israel Hits Iranian Targets Despite Trump Warning to Netanyahu
item.description: Israel struck military targets in western and central Iran despite U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly telling Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to refrain from further attacks. Trump stated Netanyahu would have to accept any US-Iran deal.

item.title: ⚠️ Tsunami Alerts Across Asia After Philippines Quake, Evacuations Underway
item.description: Tsunami warnings were issued for Indonesia, the Philippines, and Japan after a powerful earthquake off Mindanao. Officials warned of possible waves over one meter as coastal communities evacuated to higher ground.

item.title: 🚀⚔️🇮🇷 IRGC Claims Israel Used Air-Launched Ballistic Missiles in Iran Attack
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stated that Israel employed air-launched ballistic missiles to strike targets inside Iran, marking a significant escalation in military capabilities used in the conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesian Political Heavyweights Position as Prabowo Falters
item.description: Indonesia's next presidential election is not until 2029, but political heavyweights are already maneuvering for position amid signs of weakness in the current administration.
